evals.prompt: My machine vision lens is f/2.8, 25 mm focal length. On-axis the dot pattern is tight, but at the corners the spots look like asymmetric comets pointing toward the center. What aberrations should I suspect first, and what are two optical and two setup-level mitigations?
evals.expected_output: Coma and possibly astigmatism off-axis; mention field angle, stop position/symmetry, decenter, stopping down tradeoff vs diffraction, sensor alignment.

evals.prompt: Explain longitudinal vs lateral chromatic aberration in two sentences each, then say when a monochrome LED removes the issue entirely vs when an apochromat is still worth it.
evals.expected_output: Clear axial vs transverse distinction; LED removes chromatic classes for single-wavelength use; apo for broadband small pixels or color imaging.

evals.prompt: Why does stopping down improve spherical aberration only up to a point, and what begins to dominate if I keep closing the iris?
evals.expected_output: Marginal ray height/angle reduction; eventually diffraction (Airy) limits MTF and spot size.
